                    ### Local Classes  
                    - SleepAssessment: A process or system used to evaluate the quality and patterns of an individual's sleep, often by measuring various parameters such as duration, depth, and disturbances.
- SleepStage: A distinct period of sleep characterized by specific patterns of brain and muscle activity, as well as other measurable physiological variables. Sleep stages are typically identified during a sleep cycle and include stages of light sleep, deep sleep, and REM (Rapid Eye Movement) sleep.
- SleepCycle: A full cycle of sleep that progresses through various stages of sleep, including REM and non-REM stages, and is repeated multiple times throughout the night. Each cycle typically lasts about 90 minutes.
- SleepPattern: The characteristic sequence of sleep stages and cycles that an individual experiences over the course of a night. Sleep patterns can vary based on age, health, and other factors.
- SleepQuality: A measure of how well an individual slept during a given period, often assessed by the proportion of time spent in restorative deep sleep versus lighter stages of sleep, and the number of awakenings or disturbances experienced.
- SleepDisturbance: Any interruption or disruption of sleep, such as waking up during the night, which can affect the overall quality and restorative value of sleep.
- SleepDuration: The total amount of time spent sleeping, typically measured over a 24-hour period, which can be influenced by sleep patterns and sleep quality.
- SleepPosition: The physical position in which an individual sleeps, which can include lying on the back, side, or stomach, and can influence sleep quality and comfort.

                    ### Source Text
                    Lead: This article describes the different parameters in the "Activity & Sleep" algorithm. SENS motion can, among others, be used to provide an objective assessment of sleep quality

## General

This article describes the different parameters in the "Activity & Sleep" algorithm

SENS motion can, among others, be used to provide an objective assessment of sleep quality. This is done with the "Activity & Sleep" algorithm, which is based on measuring movement during sleep, sleep quality and sleeping position

The algorithm is built to find the time where a patient goes to sleep between 6 PM and 1 AM, and the time where a patient wakes up between 5 AM and 12 PM. The algorithm only determines sleep at night, and does not regard naps during the day as sleep. Therefore the algorithm will not work for patients with irregular sleeping schedules

*For a description of the general activity categories, please refer to this article: [Description of activity categories](/support/web-application/description-of-activity-categories)*

## Description of Sleep Parameters

The "Activity & Sleep" algorithm assesses movements during sleep, duration of sleep and sleeping position. The following table describes the different parameters

The categorization of movement relies on "intensity count". Read more about intensity count in this article: [Description of activity categories](/support/web-application/description-of-activity-categories)

**Bold:** categories as seen on the web-app ([app.sens.dk](https://app.sens.dk))

*Italics:* categories as seen on an exported CSV-file

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| **Duration of sleep** | Estimates when the patient sleeps (time between going to sleep and waking up)

The patient is estimated to have gone to bed the last time the patient is up and walking for more than 1 minute between 6 PM and 1 AM

The patient is estimated to have woken up the first time the patient is up and walking for more than 30 seconds between 5 AM and 12 PM. |
| **Sleep No Movement**
*sleep_no_movement* | The patient lies horizontally (+- 45 degrees) without movement. Movements with intensity count under 2 are also categorized as Sleep No Movement. |
| **Sleep Movement**
*sleep_movement* | The patient lies horizontally (+- 45 degrees) with minimal to moderate movements

Movements with an intensity count over 2 is categorized as Sleep Movement

The movement is quantified with +30 seconds before and after the movement. For instance, a movement lasting 10 seconds will be assigned this category with +30 seconds before and after the movement. This means a total of 1 minute and 10 seconds categorized as Sleep Movement

Note that movements in this category usually happen during light sleep. |
| **Sleep Active**
*sleep_active* | The patient is standing vertically (+- 45 degrees)

The movement is categorized as Sleep Active regardless of whether the patient is moving or not, as long as the patient is standing upright. |

The information above is based on these references

J. B. Webster et al. *An Activity Based Sleep Monitor System for Ambulatory Use.*
J. D. van der Berg et al. *Identifying waking time in 24 h accelerometry data in adults using an automated Algorithm.*
Input from Professor Poul Jennum from Glostrup Hospital

Below you see an example of the "Activity & Sleep" algorithm applied on a real measurement

Image: Activity & Sleep algorithm example (source: /img/support/web-application/description-of-sleep-parameters/sleep-algorithm-example.png)

                    Lead: This article describes the different parameters in the "Activity & Sleep" algorithm. SENS motion can, among others, be used to provide an objective assessment of sleep quality

## General

This article describes the different parameters in the "Activity & Sleep" algorithm

SENS motion can, among others, be used to provide an objective assessment of sleep quality. This is done with the "Activity & Sleep" algorithm, which is based on measuring movement during sleep, sleep quality and sleeping position

The algorithm is built to find the time where a patient goes to sleep between 6 PM and 1 AM, and the time where a patient wakes up between 5 AM and 12 PM. The algorithm only determines sleep at night, and does not regard naps during the day as sleep. Therefore the algorithm will not work for patients with irregular sleeping schedules

*For a description of the general activity categories, please refer to this article: [Description of activity categories](/support/web-application/description-of-activity-categories)*

## Description of Sleep Parameters

The "Activity & Sleep" algorithm assesses movements during sleep, duration of sleep and sleeping position. The following table describes the different parameters

The categorization of movement relies on "intensity count". Read more about intensity count in this article: [Description of activity categories](/support/web-application/description-of-activity-categories)

**Bold:** categories as seen on the web-app ([app.sens.dk](https://app.sens.dk))

*Italics:* categories as seen on an exported CSV-file

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| **Duration of sleep** | Estimates when the patient sleeps (time between going to sleep and waking up)

The patient is estimated to have gone to bed the last time the patient is up and walking for more than 1 minute between 6 PM and 1 AM

The patient is estimated to have woken up the first time the patient is up and walking for more than 30 seconds between 5 AM and 12 PM. |
| **Sleep No Movement**
*sleep_no_movement* | The patient lies horizontally (+- 45 degrees) without movement. Movements with intensity count under 2 are also categorized as Sleep No Movement. |
| **Sleep Movement**
*sleep_movement* | The patient lies horizontally (+- 45 degrees) with minimal to moderate movements

Movements with an intensity count over 2 is categorized as Sleep Movement

The movement is quantified with +30 seconds before and after the movement. For instance, a movement lasting 10 seconds will be assigned this category with +30 seconds before and after the movement. This means a total of 1 minute and 10 seconds categorized as Sleep Movement

Note that movements in this category usually happen during light sleep. |
| **Sleep Active**
*sleep_active* | The patient is standing vertically (+- 45 degrees)

The movement is categorized as Sleep Active regardless of whether the patient is moving or not, as long as the patient is standing upright. |

The information above is based on these references

J. B. Webster et al. *An Activity Based Sleep Monitor System for Ambulatory Use.*
J. D. van der Berg et al. *Identifying waking time in 24 h accelerometry data in adults using an automated Algorithm.*
Input from Professor Poul Jennum from Glostrup Hospital

Below you see an example of the "Activity & Sleep" algorithm applied on a real measurement

Image: Activity & Sleep algorithm example (source: /img/support/web-application/description-of-sleep-parameters/sleep-algorithm-example.png)

                    ### Valid Properties
                    - hasPart: Relates an entity to its components or parts. (e.g., A car hasPart an engine).
- isPartOf: The inverse of hasPart. Relates a component to the whole. (e.g., An engine isPart of a car).
- participatesIn: Relates an object to an event or process it is involved in. (e.g., A person participatesIn a meeting).
- hasParticipant: The inverse of participatesIn. Relates an event to the objects involved in it. (e.g., A meeting hasParticipant a person).
- hasQuality: Relates an entity to an attribute, characteristic, or value it possesses. (e.g., A car hasQuality color).
- realizes: Relates a physical manifestation to the abstract information it represents. (e.g., A printed book realizes a text; A physical statue realizes a 3D design).
- classifies: Relates a concept, category, or role to the actual entity it describes. (e.g., The role 'Teacher' classifies a specific Person).
- hasLocation: Relates an object or event to the spatial region where it is located or occurs. (e.g., A meeting hasLocation 'Conference Room A').
- hasTime: Relates an event or process to the specific time period during which it occurs. (e.g., A concert hasTime 'Saturday Night').
- isAbout: Relates an information object (like data, text, or a picture) to the entity it represents or describes. (e.g., A photograph isAbout a landscape).
- hasComponent: Relates a complex system or platform to its individual components. (e.g., A computer hasComponent a motherboard).
- isComponentOf: The inverse of hasComponent. Relates a component to the system it belongs to. (e.g., A motherboard isComponentOf a computer).
- precedes: A sequence relation indicating that one event, process, or time happens before another. (e.g., 'Writing' precedes 'Publishing').
- follows: The inverse of precedes. Indicates one event or process comes after another. (e.g., 'Publishing' follows 'Writing').
- hasRole: Relates an object to the function or role it performs. (e.g., A person hasRole 'Manager').
- hasDataValue: A data property that assigns a specific numeric or literal value to an entity or its quality. (e.g., A WeightQuality hasDataValue '75.5').
- hasMember: Relates a collection, dataset, or group to the individual items it contains. (e.g., A Library hasMember 'Book').
- isMemberOf: The inverse of hasMember. Relates an item to the collection it belongs to. (e.g., A 'Book' isMemberOf a Library).
- expresses: Relates a piece of information or data to the abstract concept it means or represents. (e.g., A spoken word expresses a concept).
- hasPrecondition: Direct precedence indicating a requirement. Relates an action to another action or event that must happen first. (e.g., 'Buying a Ticket' is a precondition for 'Boarding a Train').
- hasPostcondition: Direct succession indicating a result. Relates an action to the outcome or event that necessarily follows it. (e.g., 'Getting Wet' is a postcondition of 'Swimming').
- executesTask: Relates an action or physical event to the specific conceptual task or goal it is designed to accomplish. (e.g., A reading event executesTask 'Learning').
- actsThrough: Relates an entity to the medium or tool it uses to interact or perform an action. (e.g., A writer actsThrough a 'Pen').
- satisfies: Relates an entity or action to a rule, plan, or requirement that it successfully fulfills. (e.g., A building process satisfies a 'Blueprint').

                    ### Source Text
                    Lead: GeneralThis article contains a

General
This article contains a guide to editing participants' information, including the monitoring period. "Participant" refers to the person wearing the SENS motion sensor
To create a new participant, see
this article
Edit participant
To edit a participant's information, like name or monitor period, click the three dots to the right of a participant and select "Edit participant"
Select "Delete particpant" to delete a participant
Participant information
Participant information contains information about the participant
Particpant ID/First name does not need to be personally identifiable, and it is optional to enter last name and phone number
"Timezone" describes the timezone in which the participant has worn the sensor
Sensor information
"Measurement Method" is set to a standard algorithm by default. If you wish to use other algorithms or a dual sensor solution, please
contact SENS
Select or change a participant's sensor in the "Sensor on Thigh" drop down. Available sensors are marked with a green dot before the sensor ID and a green person after the sensor ID
If you wish to have multiple measurements from different sensors connected to the same participant, you need another set-up, kindly contact support for further information
If a sensor's battery has run out during a measurement, or a sensor is lost during the monitor period, you must create a new "participant" and assign a new sensor. The data from the two sensors can then be concatenated during data processing
Monitor period
Select the start- and end time for the monitor period in this modal. The period can always be adjusted, and the defined monitor period does not interfere with the stored data
There are 3 options for selecting a start time
Now
The monitor period will start now
Specific time
Define the final monitor period by inputting a specific start- and end time. You must select both a time and date
Not Defined Yet
The monitor period is not yet known. In this case it is important to return to the particpant and manually input the monitor period
For the
end time
you can select "Specify duration" in addition to the options above. Here you can specify the expected number of days, the measurement will last. When the measurement is complete you must adjust the monitor period accordingly
Beware that you can not select an end time if you have not defined a start time
You can not export PDF reports if the start- or end time is not defined yet

                    Lead: Background This article explains how data in an exported CSV file can be made readable in Excel. See this article for a guide on how to

Background
This article explains how data in an exported CSV file can be made readable in Excel
See
this article
for a guide on how to export data from a sensor in CSV format
Making a CSV file readable
After you have downloaded a CSV file from the web app, it can be opened with Excel. The format of the file is not formatted for readability by default. Some settings need to be adjusted to get an overview of the data. Follow this guide to make these adjustments
1. Locate your CSV file and open it with Excel. When first opened, the file will look like this
2. Mark column A (1) and enter the Data tab in the top menu (Alt + A)
3. Select "Text to Columns". This will open a pop-up modal
4. Select "Delimited"and click "Next"
5. Tick off "Tab" and "Comma". Proceed by clicking "Next"
6. Click on "Advanced"
7. Set "Decimal separator" to " . " (dot) and "Thousands separator" to " , " (comma). Click "OK"
8. Click "Finish"
The sheet has now been formatted for better readability and will look like this

file_path qhhh')

while True

if len(d) == 0
break

#ts, x, y, z = s.unpack(b'\0\0' + d)
i += 1
return data
```

                    ### Valid Properties
                    - hasPart: Relates an entity to its components or parts. (e.g., A car hasPart an engine).

- participatesIn: Relates an object to an event or process it is involved in. (e.g., A person participatesIn a meeting).
- hasParticipant: The inverse of participatesIn. Relates an event to the objects involved in it. (e.g., A meeting hasParticipant a person).
- hasQuality: Relates an entity to an attribute, characteristic, or value it possesses. (e.g., A car hasQuality color).
- realizes: Relates a physical manifestation to the abstract information it represents. (e.g., A printed book realizes a text; A physical statue realizes a 3D design).
- classifies: Relates a concept, category, or role to the actual entity it describes. (e.g., The role 'Teacher' classifies a specific Person).
- hasLocation: Relates an object or event to the spatial region where it is located or occurs. (e.g., A meeting hasLocation 'Conference Room A').
- hasTime: Relates an event or process to the specific time period during which it occurs. (e.g., A concert hasTime 'Saturday Night').
- isAbout: Relates an information object (like data, text, or a picture) to the entity it represents or describes. (e.g., A photograph isAbout a landscape).
- hasComponent: Relates a complex system or platform to its individual components. (e.g., A computer hasComponent a motherboard).
- isComponentOf: The inverse of hasComponent. Relates a component to the system it belongs to. (e.g., A motherboard isComponentOf a computer).
- precedes: A sequence relation indicating that one event, process, or time happens before another. (e.g., 'Writing' precedes 'Publishing').
- follows: The inverse of precedes. Indicates one event or process comes after another. (e.g., 'Publishing' follows 'Writing').
- hasRole: Relates an object to the function or role it performs. (e.g., A person hasRole 'Manager').

- hasPostcondition: Direct succession indicating a result. Relates an action to the outcome or event that necessarily follows it. (e.g., 'Getting Wet' is a postcondition of 'Swimming').
- executesTask: Relates an action or physical event to the specific conceptual task or goal it is designed to accomplish. (e.g., A reading event executesTask 'Learning').

                    ### Source Text
                    We're excited to introduce version 3.9 with major improvements to navigation, sensor management, and file handling. This release focuses on making your workflow more efficient and providing better visibility into your sensor data What's New in 3.9 Reorganised Navigation – Three main sections (Workspace, Sensors, Files) for easier access to key features Enhanced Breadcrumbs – Quick navigation between project and participant views Improved Sensor Overview – Battery level info, sensor state, pending actions, and bulk operations Battery Lifetime Estimation – See estimated days remaining for each sensor battery Streamlined File Export – Export multiple files without closing the menu Expandable Participant Tables – View detailed information without leaving the table Memory Status Indicators – Quickly identify sensors with memory issues ActiMotus 2.1 Algorithm Support – Multi-sensor measurements and enhanced diary features Participant Details Enhancements – New Steps section added Security Improvements – Stricter password policies and enhanced two-factor authentication Expanded Country Support – More countries available for selection Streamlined UI – Overall interface improvements for better usability Reorganised Main Navigation Sections New Feature We've reorganised the main navigation to make it easier to find what you need Three dedicated sections make up the main navigation Workspace – Manage your projects and participants all in one place Sensors – View and manage all your sensors with enhanced battery and status information Files – Monitor and download your exported files What you need to know These sections replace the previous navigation structure and provide a more intuitive way to access your most-used features. You'll find all project and participant management tools in Workspace, sensor monitoring in Sensors, and file exports in Files. Additionally, the change organisation menu is now located directly in the header, next to profile settings, making it quicker to switch between organisations Enhanced Navigation with Breadcrumbs New Feature Breadcrumb navigation has been added throughout the application to help you understand where you are and quickly navigate between related views What you need to know When viewing project or participant details, you'll see breadcrumbs at the top showing your current location. You can click on any breadcrumb to jump directly to that section—for example, from a participant's detail page back to the participant overview or project list. This makes it much faster to navigate between related pages without using the back button How to use it Simply click on any item in the breadcrumb trail to navigate to that level. The breadcrumbs automatically update based on your current location Sensor Overview Improvements Battery Level Information New Feature The sensor overview now displays comprehensive battery information to help you monitor sensor lifetime Each sensor now shows Battery voltage – The current voltage reading from the sensor (when hovering over the icon) Estimated days left – A calculation of how many days of battery life remain based on the sensor's runtime and battery What you need to know The estimated days left is calculated based on how long the sensor has been active and its current battery voltage. This helps you plan ahead for sensor replacements and ensures you don't lose data due to depleted batteries How to use it Look for the battery icon and information under each sensor in the Sensors section. The estimated days left will help you identify sensors that need attention soon. To see battery voltage, hover the mouse cursor over the battery icon Sensor State and Pending Actions New Feature The sensor overview now clearly displays both the current sensor state and any pending actions What you need to know Sensor State shows what the sensor is currently doing (on, off, etc.) based on the last time it connected to the mobile app Pending Action shows any requested action (like "turn on" or "turn off") that is waiting for the sensor to connect to the mobile app This dual display helps you understand both the current status and what will happen next when the sensor syncs How to use it Check the sensor state column to see current status, and the pending action column to see what's queued. Pending actions will be completed automatically when the sensor connects to the SENS motion mobile app Improved Sensor Action Accessibility New Feature Turning sensors on and off is

Define the lateral relationships (ObjectProperties) between the provided Local Classes based on the Source Text.

### Terminology
**Class**: A group, collection, category or set of entities sharing characteristics or attributes.
**ObjectProperty**: A relationship that links two classes together.
**Domain**: The subject or "owner" class of the property.
**Range**: The object or "target" class of the property.
**Local Class**: A newly extracted domain specific class.

### Guidelines
1. **NO Hierarchies**: DO NOT extract "is-a", SubClassOf, Equivalent, or Disjoint relationships. Focus ONLY on lateral links.
2. **Strict Vocabulary**: You may ONLY use the exact names of the classes listed in the provided 'Local Classes' block for Domains and Ranges.
3. **Class Interaction**: You must ONLY link the provided Local Classes to each other.
4. **Single Triple**: Output completed relation triples linking one Local Class to another.
5. **Explicit Only**: Only extract links aggressively stated in the Source Text. DO NOT invent or infer connections.
6. **No Duplicates**: Never repeat the exact same relationship.
7. **Stop generating** immediately when you have extracted all explicitly stated relations.

### Example

                    ### Local Classes  
                    - FeatureUpdate: A new feature or improvement added to a system, application, or service that enhances its functionality or user experience.
- BulkAction: An action that can be performed on multiple items or entities simultaneously, often leading to increased efficiency in processing or management.
- BulkEdit: A feature that allows users to apply changes or perform actions on a selection of items at once, typically found in software applications for bulk data management.
- BatteryLifetimeEstimation: A feature that provides an estimation of the remaining battery life for a device or sensor, often used to predict when a device will need recharging or replacement.
- FileExportWorkflow: A process or set of features related to the exporting of files, which may include batch operations or streamlined user interfaces to facilitate the transfer of data.
- ProjectManagementImprovements: Enhancements or new features in project management tools that simplify the creation, organization, and tracking of projects, often aimed at improving user experience and efficiency.
- ParticipantTableEnhancements: Improvements or additional features added to a table or data table interface that provide more information or functionality, such as the ability to expand rows for more details.
- MemoryStatusIndicator: A feature that displays the memory status of a device or sensor, providing users with information about memory usage or availability.

                    ### Valid Properties
                    - hasPart: Relates an entity to its components or parts. (e.g., A car hasPart an engine).
- isPartOf: The inverse of hasPart. Relates a component to the whole. (e.g., An engine isPart of a car).
- participatesIn: Relates an object to an event or process it is involved in. (e.g., A person participatesIn a meeting).
- hasParticipant: The inverse of participatesIn. Relates an event to the objects involved in it. (e.g., A meeting hasParticipant a person).
- hasQuality: Relates an entity to an attribute, characteristic, or value it possesses. (e.g., A car hasQuality color).
- realizes: Relates a physical manifestation to the abstract information it represents. (e.g., A printed book realizes a text; A physical statue realizes a 3D design).
- classifies: Relates a concept, category, or role to the actual entity it describes. (e.g., The role 'Teacher' classifies a specific Person).
- hasLocation: Relates an object or event to the spatial region where it is located or occurs. (e.g., A meeting hasLocation 'Conference Room A').
- hasTime: Relates an event or process to the specific time period during which it occurs. (e.g., A concert hasTime 'Saturday Night').
- isAbout: Relates an information object (like data, text, or a picture) to the entity it represents or describes. (e.g., A photograph isAbout a landscape).
- hasComponent: Relates a complex system or platform to its individual components. (e.g., A computer hasComponent a motherboard).
- isComponentOf: The inverse of hasComponent. Relates a component to the system it belongs to. (e.g., A motherboard isComponentOf a computer).
- precedes: A sequence relation indicating that one event, process, or time happens before another. (e.g., 'Writing' precedes 'Publishing').
- follows: The inverse of precedes. Indicates one event or process comes after another. (e.g., 'Publishing' follows 'Writing').
- hasRole: Relates an object to the function or role it performs. (e.g., A person hasRole 'Manager').
- hasDataValue: A data property that assigns a specific numeric or literal value to an entity or its quality. (e.g., A WeightQuality hasDataValue '75.5').
- hasMember: Relates a collection, dataset, or group to the individual items it contains. (e.g., A Library hasMember 'Book').
- isMemberOf: The inverse of hasMember. Relates an item to the collection it belongs to. (e.g., A 'Book' isMemberOf a Library).
- expresses: Relates a piece of information or data to the abstract concept it means or represents. (e.g., A spoken word expresses a concept).
- hasPrecondition: Direct precedence indicating a requirement. Relates an action to another action or event that must happen first. (e.g., 'Buying a Ticket' is a precondition for 'Boarding a Train').
- hasPostcondition: Direct succession indicating a result. Relates an action to the outcome or event that necessarily follows it. (e.g., 'Getting Wet' is a postcondition of 'Swimming').
- executesTask: Relates an action or physical event to the specific conceptual task or goal it is designed to accomplish. (e.g., A reading event executesTask 'Learning').
- actsThrough: Relates an entity to the medium or tool it uses to interact or perform an action. (e.g., A writer actsThrough a 'Pen').
- satisfies: Relates an entity or action to a rule, plan, or requirement that it successfully fulfills. (e.g., A building process satisfies a 'Blueprint').

                    now more easily accessible from the sensor overview page, and pending actions are now explicitly displayed What you need to know Sensor on/off actions are now available directly from the actions dropdown in the sensor overview table, making them quicker to access How to use it Use the actions dropdown (three dots) in the sensor overview table to turn sensors on or off. After initiating an action, you'll see it displayed in the "Pending Action" column. The action will be completed automatically when the sensor connects to the SENS motion mobile app. You can cancel pending actions from the actions dropdown if needed Bulk Actions New Feature Bulk actions are now available at the top of the sensor overview, making it easy to manage multiple sensors at once What you need to know You can now select multiple sensors and perform actions on all of them simultaneously Turn multiple sensors on or off Cancel pending actions for multiple sensors How to use it Click the "Bulk Edit" button in the toolbar at the top of the Sensors page. Select the sensors you want to modify, then choose your action from the bulk actions toolbar that appears Battery Lifetime Estimation New Feature The system now calculates and displays estimated battery lifetime for each sensor What you need to know The estimated battery lifetime shows how many days are left before the sensor's battery is expected to run out. This calculation is based on How long the sensor has been active The current battery voltage Expected battery consumption patterns Sensor batteries are typically supported for 120 days. After this period, sensors may stop working at any moment How to use it Monitor the "Est. X days left" indicator in the sensor overview. When this number gets low (especially below 10 days), do not use the sensor for any measurements meant to run for longer Improved File Export Workflow New Feature The file export process has been streamlined to make it easier to export multiple files without navigating back and forth What you need to know When you send a file to export, the export menu no longer closes automatically. Instead, a helpful success popup appears with a direct link to the Files Overview page. This means you can Export multiple files for the same participant in quick succession Continue working in the export menu without interruption Click the link in the popup to go directly to your Files Overview when ready How to use it After selecting your export options and clicking export, you'll see a success notification with a "Files Overview" link. Click it to view your queued files, or continue exporting more files—the menu stays open for your convenience Project Management Improvements User-Created Projects New Feature Users can now create new projects directly within the Workspace, removing the need for administrative setup What you need to know This update makes it significantly easier to manage larger studies, onboarding new cohorts, or running multiple project streams at once. Previously, project creation required assistance from administrators or support. With this release, any authorised user can set up projects independently How to use it Navigate to the Workspace section and click Add Project From there, you can enter project details, assign participants, and configure settings as needed. Permissions continue to follow organisational roles to ensure project creation remains secure and controlled Participant Table Enhancements Expandable Rows New Feature Participant table rows can now be expanded to reveal additional information without navigating to a separate details page What you need to know Each participant row can be expanded to show Additional participant information Measurement details Sensor information Status messages You can expand individual rows by clicking the expand icon, or use the "Expand All" button in the table header to expand all rows at once for a comprehensive view How to use it Look for the expand/collapse icon (chevron) in the first column of the participant table. Click it to expand that row, or click the icon in the header to expand or collapse all rows simultaneously Memory Status Indicator New Feature A new memory status column has been added to the participant table to help you quickly identify sensors with memory issues What

                    ### Local Classes  
                    - DataStorageRequirement: A condition or need for a device or system to store data, which, when reached, necessitates synchronization to free up space.
- DataCollectionPeriod: A specific time frame during which data is gathered or recorded, often used to organize and reference data collection events.
- DataFilteringFeature: A functionality that allows users to sift through data based on certain criteria, such as date ranges or specific attributes.
- DataCorrelationTool: A tool or feature that enables the association of different data points, such as sensor readings and diary entries, to provide a more comprehensive analysis.
- DataAnalysisTool: A system or application feature that processes and interprets data, often to provide insights or to support decision-making.
- DataPrivacyPolicy: A set of rules and guidelines that govern the collection, storage, and sharing of personal data to ensure user privacy and compliance with regulations.
- DataSecurityFeature: Technological measures implemented to protect data from unauthorized access, alteration, or theft, often including encryption and access controls.
- DataSynchronizationProcess: The act of making sure that data across different systems or devices is consistent and up-to-date, often necessary when storage capacity is full.
- DataRetentionPolicy: A policy that defines how long data should be kept before it is disposed of or archived, balancing the need for historical records against storage costs and privacy concerns.
- DataIntegrityCheck: The process of verifying that data has not been tampered with and is accurate and reliable for use in decision-making or analysis.
- DataVisualizationTool: A software tool that converts data into a visual format, such as graphs or charts, to make it easier to understand and interpret.
- DataEntryTask: The act of manually or automatically inputting data into a system, which can be a critical step in the data collection and analysis process.
- DataEntryError: An error or discrepancy that occurs during the data entry process, which can lead to inaccurate data analysis if not identified and corrected.
- DataEntryValidation: The process of checking data for accuracy and completeness before it is entered into a system, to ensure the integrity of the data collected.
- DataEntryInterface: The user interface elements that facilitate the entry of data into a system, which can include forms, fields, and input fields.
- DataEntryGuidelines: Standards or best practices that guide the process of entering data into a system, aimed at maintaining consistency and accuracy.
- DataEntryTool: A specific application or software that assists users in entering data into a system, often designed to streamline the process and reduce errors.
- DataEntryProcedure: The method or set of steps followed when entering data into a system, which can vary depending on the system and the nature of the data.
- DataEntryProcess: The overall process of collecting, entering, and managing data within a system, from initial collection to final storage and analysis.
- DataEntryRequirement: The necessity for certain data to be entered into a system, which can be a critical component of data collection and analysis.
- DataEntryRequirement: A specific need or demand for data entry, which can drive the design and implementation of data collection systems.
- DataEntryRequirement: A formal need for data to be entered into a system, often based on regulatory, business, or research requirements.

X used (of 10)" to give you a clear picture of memory utilisation Measurement Start Date Filter New Feature You can now filter participants by measurement start date, making it easier to find and manage participants who started on the same day What you need to know The measurement start date filter allows you to quickly identify and group participants based on when their measurements began. This is particularly useful for Managing cohorts of participants who started together Tracking progress for participants who began on specific dates Organising participants by measurement start timeline How to use it Click the Expand button on the right top of the participant overview toolbar. Use the date filter to select a specific measurement start date. The table will automatically update to show only participants whose measurements started on that date, making it easy to find and manage participants who started on the same day ActiMotus Support New Feature Enhanced support for ActiMotus algorithm and project-specific questionnaires ActiMotus 2.1 Algorithm Support New Feature The system now supports the ActiMotus 2.1 algorithm, enabling multi-sensor measurements for more comprehensive data collection What you need to know The ActiMotus 2.1 algorithm allows you to use multiple sensors simultaneously for a single measurement, providing richer data and more accurate results. This is particularly useful for complex studies that require data from multiple sensor sources How to use it When setting up measurements, you can now configure multi-sensor setups that leverage the ActiMotus 2.1 algorithm. The system will automatically process data from all configured sensors together Motus Diary Under Participant Details New Feature Motus diary entries are now accessible directly from the participant details page What you need to know You can now view and manage Motus diary entries for each participant without needing to export. This provides a more integrated view of participant data, combining sensor measurements with diary entries in one place How to use it Navigate to a participant's details page and look for the Diary section. Here you'll find all Motus diary entries associated with that participant, making it easier to correlate diary data with sensor measurements Participant Details Page Enhancements Steps Section New Feature A new Steps section has been added to the participant details page What you need to know The Steps section provides a detailed view of step count data for participants. This information helps you track participant activity levels and correlate them with other measurement data How to use it Navigate to a participant's details page and choose the Steps view in the graph dropdown. Here you'll see step count for each interval in the day Overall Improvements Streamlined UI New Feature The overall user interface has been streamlined for better usability and a cleaner appearance What you need to know Throughout the application, we've made numerous UI improvements including Cleaner layouts with better spacing More intuitive button placement Improved visual hierarchy Enhanced colour schemes for better readability Smoother transitions and animations How to use it These improvements are visible throughout the application. You'll notice a more polished and consistent experience across all pages and features Security Improvements Stricter Password Policies New Feature Administrators can now enforce stricter password policies for enhanced security What you need to know New password policy options allow you to require Longer minimum password lengths More complex password requirements (uppercase, lowercase, numbers, special characters) Password expiration policies Password history requirements to prevent reuse How to use it Please contact our support to make sure your project is configured for the correct policies Enhanced Country Support New Feature The list of available countries has been expanded What you need

You are an Ontology Object Property Extractor.

### Goal

### Terminology
**Class**: A group, collection, category or set of entities sharing characteristics or attributes.
**ObjectProperty**: A relationship that links two classes together.
**Domain**: The subject or "owner" class of the property.
**Range**: The object or "target" class of the property.
**Local Class**: A newly extracted domain specific class.

### Guidelines
1. **NO Hierarchies**: DO NOT extract "is-a", SubClassOf, Equivalent, or Disjoint relationships. Focus ONLY on lateral links.
2. **Strict Vocabulary**: You may ONLY use the exact names of the classes listed in the provided 'Local Classes' block for Domains and Ranges.
3. **Class Interaction**: You must ONLY link the provided Local Classes to each other.
4. **Single Triple**: Output completed relation triples linking one Local Class to another.
5. **Explicit Only**: Only extract links aggressively stated in the Source Text. DO NOT invent or infer connections.
6. **No Duplicates**: Never repeat the exact same relationship.
7. **Stop generating** immediately when you have extracted all explicitly stated relations.

                    ### Local Classes  
                    - ApplicationFeature: A specific functionality or component within a software application that provides a particular service or capability to the user.
- SecurityFeature: A set of measures or capabilities within a system designed to protect data, ensure privacy, and prevent unauthorized access.
- UserInterface: The means by which a person interacts with a digital device or a software application, including the display, input, and output elements.
- UserFeedback: Comments, suggestions, or evaluations provided by users regarding their experience with a product or service, often used to inform future development and improvements.
- DataCollectionTool: A method or system used to gather information, typically from users or participants, for analysis or research purposes.
- UserProfile: A digital representation of a user's personal information and preferences within an application, often used for customization and personalization of services.
- UserProfileFeature: A specific aspect or component of a UserProfile that enhances the user's experience by providing additional functionality or personalization.

                    ### Valid Properties
                    - hasPart: Relates an entity to its components or parts. (e.g., A car hasPart an engine).
- isPartOf: The inverse of hasPart. Relates a component to the whole. (e.g., An engine isPart of a car).
- participatesIn: Relates an object to an event or process it is involved in. (e.g., A person participatesIn a meeting).
- hasParticipant: The inverse of participatesIn. Relates an event to the objects involved in it. (e.g., A meeting hasParticipant a person).
- hasQuality: Relates an entity to an attribute, characteristic, or value it possesses. (e.g., A car hasQuality color).
- realizes: Relates a physical manifestation to the abstract information it represents. (e.g., A printed book realizes a text; A physical statue realizes a 3D design).
- classifies: Relates a concept, category, or role to the actual entity it describes. (e.g., The role 'Teacher' classifies a specific Person).
- hasLocation: Relates an object or event to the spatial region where it is located or occurs. (e.g., A meeting hasLocation 'Conference Room A').
- hasTime: Relates an event or process to the specific time period during which it occurs. (e.g., A concert hasTime 'Saturday Night').
- isAbout: Relates an information object (like data, text, or a picture) to the entity it represents or describes. (e.g., A photograph isAbout a landscape).
- hasComponent: Relates a complex system or platform to its individual components. (e.g., A computer hasComponent a motherboard).
- isComponentOf: The inverse of hasComponent. Relates a component to the system it belongs to. (e.g., A motherboard isComponentOf a computer).
- precedes: A sequence relation indicating that one event, process, or time happens before another. (e.g., 'Writing' precedes 'Publishing').
- follows: The inverse of precedes. Indicates one event or process comes after another. (e.g., 'Publishing' follows 'Writing').
- hasRole: Relates an object to the function or role it performs. (e.g., A person hasRole 'Manager').
- hasDataValue: A data property that assigns a specific numeric or literal value to an entity or its quality. (e.g., A WeightQuality hasDataValue '75.5').
- hasMember: Relates a collection, dataset, or group to the individual items it contains. (e.g., A Library hasMember 'Book').
- isMemberOf: The inverse of hasMember. Relates an item to the collection it belongs to. (e.g., A 'Book' isMemberOf a Library).
- expresses: Relates a piece of information or data to the abstract concept it means or represents. (e.g., A spoken word expresses a concept).
- hasPrecondition: Direct precedence indicating a requirement. Relates an action to another action or event that must happen first. (e.g., 'Buying a Ticket' is a precondition for 'Boarding a Train').
- hasPostcondition: Direct succession indicating a result. Relates an action to the outcome or event that necessarily follows it. (e.g., 'Getting Wet' is a postcondition of 'Swimming').
- executesTask: Relates an action or physical event to the specific conceptual task or goal it is designed to accomplish. (e.g., A reading event executesTask 'Learning').
- actsThrough: Relates an entity to the medium or tool it uses to interact or perform an action. (e.g., A writer actsThrough a 'Pen').
- satisfies: Relates an entity or action to a rule, plan, or requirement that it successfully fulfills. (e.g., A building process satisfies a 'Blueprint').

                    ### Source Text
                    Lead: [](https://support.sens.dk/hc/en-us/articles/32478651584925-Web-App-3-9-Release-Notes)

## Background

Each sensor can be in different 'states'. In order for the sensor to be turned on and ready for monitoring, the sensor must first be turned on and be synced with the SENS motion app

The sensor overview clearly displays both the current sensor state and any pending actions
Image: Sensor state (source: /img/support/web-application/turn-sensor-on-off/pending-action-status.png)

**Sensor State** shows what the sensor is currently doing (on, off, etc.) based on the last time it connected to the SENS motion app
**Pending Action** shows any requested action (like "turn on" or "turn off") that is waiting for the sensor to connect to the SENS motion app

This dual display helps you understand both the current status and what will happen next when the sensor syncs with the SENS motion app
Check the sensor state column to see current status, and the pending action column to see what's queued

Pending actions will be completed automatically when the sensor connects to the participant's mobile app

We estimate how long a sensor's battery should last (default about 120 days)
We track how long it has been running and when it was last seen. If it's still running, time since last seen is counted against the battery
If we have a recent voltage reading, we adjust the remaining days based on voltage thresholds. This is so we can get a more realistic estimate, in case something has happened to the battery. Very low voltage forces the remaining days to near-zero; normal voltage leaves the estimate as-is
If the sensor hasn't been seen in a very long time (years) or has run far beyond its expected lifetime, we show **"Battery expired"** in UI
If the battery data is older than 10 days, we determine voltage is "unknown.", and only show the estimated days left (not the voltage) in the UI

## How to turn on the sensor

You can either turn the sensors on, one by one *or* in bulk. See both ways below

1. Log on to [https://app.sens.dk/r/login](https://app.sens.dk/r/login)

                    ### Turn on sensors one by one

2. Enter the "Sensors" tab, click the three dots on the far right and click "Turn sensor on"

Image: Turn on sensor one by one (source: /img/support/web-application/turn-sensor-on-off/turn-single-sensor-on.png)

### Turn on several sensors at once (bulk)

2. Click "Bulk actions", and select the sensors you want to turn on, followed by clicking "Turn on the selected sensors"

Image: Turn on several sensors at once (bulk) (source: /img/support/web-application/turn-sensor-on-off/turn-bulk-sensors-on.png)

3. The sensors will change under "Pending action" to

Image: Pending action status (source: /img/support/web-application/turn-sensor-on-off/pending-action-on.png)

4. Open the SENS motion app on your mobile/tablet (accept bluetooth and location services when you download first time)

Image: Open SENS motion app (source: /img/support/web-application/turn-sensor-on-off/sens-motion-app.jpg)

5. Shake the sensor a few times to activate the accelerometer to connect the sensor with the SENS motion app

6. Check on the website if the status has changed to "On"

**Local Class**: A newly extracted domain specific class.

### Guidelines
1. **NO Object Properties or Hierarchies**: Build ONLY data attributes (like names, IDs, dates, quantities) for classes.
2. **Domain Strictness**: You may ONLY use the exact names of the classes listed in the provided 'Local Classes' block for the Domain.

7. **Stop generating** immediately when you have extracted all explicitly stated relations.

- ResearchProject: A coordinated effort to investigate and understand phenomena, typically involving the collection and analysis of data.

text: Better Treatment. Better Research

## What is SENS motion®
title: What is SENS motion®

### System Measures
subtitle: System Measures
list: Rest time.Standing time.Walking time.Running & High Intensity Movement time.Cycling time.Steps.Intensity.Sleep time and quality
**SENS motion®** is an integrated system for collecting physical activity data from groups of people. It consists of a wireless activity sensor that automatically transfers data to a secure cloud. It is especially well suited for use in the healthcare sector and for large research projects

## SENS motion® – For Healthcare
title: SENS motion® – For Healthcare

### SENS motion® – For Healthcare consists of
subtitle: SENS motion® – For Healthcare consists of
list: The SENS motion activity sensor for use on patients.A patient app, with gamification feedback for motivating the patient to become more active.(Optional) Monitor display providing overview of patients.Access to reports of individual patients activity
**SENS motion® – For Healthcare** enables the use of SENS motion® activity sensor in healthcare environments, such as hospitals or care centres. The collected activity data can be presented to the patient itself for motivational purpose, assist healthcare professionals in treatment and help prioritize their valuable time efficiently

[Read More →](/en/healthcare/)

## SENS motion® – For Research
title: SENS motion® – For Research

### SENS motion® – For Research consists of
subtitle: SENS motion® – For Research consists of
list: The SENS motion® activity sensor.Smartphone app for automatically transferring data to the secure cloud.Web-application for managing sensors, viewing or exporting data
**SENS motion® – For Research** enables the use of SENS motion activity sensor for collecting activity data or raw accelerometer data in research projects

[Read More →](/en/research/)

SENS motion® – Connect
OEM Platform for medical sensors

text: SENS Innovation ApS

## Background
title: Background
vision_quote: Our vision is to revolutionize the way physical activity can be used as an integrated and measurable part of the treatment and prevention of numerous diseases – on an equal footing with other medicine
vision_author_name: Kasper L. Lykkegaard
vision_author_title: Co-founder and CEO
SENS Innovation ApS was founded in 2014 by Kasper L. Lykkegaard and Morten Kjærgaard. The company develops medical sensor products, based on advanced sensor technology, for monitoring of physical activity with patients

The SENS products and platforms are easy to use, flexible, scalable and present accurate monitoring of the body's position and the nature of the activity, for both the individual or even larger groups. Therefore, SENS's products and technology are a valuable tool in research trials and studies in correlation to physical activity and diseases

                    ### Local Classes
                    - HospitalStudy: A research project or investigation conducted within a hospital setting to evaluate the effectiveness of a particular intervention or treatment.
- PhysicalActivityIntervention: A program or device designed to increase physical activity levels among individuals, particularly in healthcare settings.
- PatientPopulation: A specific group of individuals characterized by shared characteristics, such as age, health condition, or location, for the purpose of medical research or healthcare delivery.
- HealthcareQualityOfLife: The standard of health and well-being of a patient or group of patients, often used as an outcome measure in healthcare studies.
- Rehabilitation: A program or therapy designed to restore physical, mental, or social abilities or skills that have been impaired, often due to illness, injury, or aging.
- HealthcareDevice: Any physical tool or piece of equipment used in the context of healthcare, such as medical instruments, machines, or devices.
- HealthcareDeviceUsage: The act of using a healthcare device, which may refer to the operation of medical equipment or the application of a medical tool.
- HealthcareDeviceUsageStatistics: Data related to the use of healthcare devices, such as frequency, duration, and outcomes of their use.

                    ### Source Text
                    ## Case Highlight
title: Case Highlight

### Bispebjerg Hospital: Increased out-of-bed time of hospitalized patients
subtitle: Bispebjerg Hospital: Increased out-of-bed time of hospitalized patients
[View video](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/et-al._2-min-og-25-sek-of-fame-hell-yeah-altid-activity-6599636858651586560-oOmm/)

Bispebjerg Hospital have in a recent study increased time out of bed for elderly hospitalised patients by 51 minutes per day using SENS motion developed by the Danish start-up SENS Innovation

Many patients are inactive 18–20 hours a day in the hospital bed, and body functions decline rapidly during hospitalisation. Therefore, rehabilitation while hospitalised is crucial to avoid loss of independency, decline of quality of life and re-hospitalisation

References

[Business Insights: A patch and an app are getting patients out of bed](https://www.businessinsights.dk/healthtech/a-patch-and-an-app-are-getting-patients-out-of-bed/)
[European Journal of Internal Medicine: Dall CH, Andersen H, Povlsen TM, Henriksen M. Evaluation of a technology assisted physical activity intervention among hospitalised patients: A randomised study. Eur J Intern Med. 2019](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31494019/)

Project supported by the Association of Danish Physiotherapists, RegionH VihTek, Bispebjerg Hospital – Department of Physical and Occupational Therapy and SENS Innovation ApS

## Clinical Evidence
title: Clinical Evidence

### SENS motion® increases physical activity for admitted patients
subtitle: SENS motion® increases physical activity for admitted patients
list: Each year more than 600.000 elderly patients above the age of 65 are hospitalised (2)|Elderly hospitalised acute patients spend on average 70–90% of their time in bed and spend only 3–5% of their time either standing or walking (1)
Research has shown that even shorter periods of inactivity cause a reduction in physical fitness and muscle mass (3). Compared to younger patients, elderly patients have reduced ability to recover after periods of physical inactivity (4). This also affects the patient's ability to perform daily living (ADL) activities as well as degrading their perceived quality of life (5)

Physical inactivity is a strong factor prolonging the disease and rehabilitation period (7), increasing the risk of sequelae leading to hospital (re)admission (6). With an increase of just 100 steps taken per day the risk of readmission is reduced with approximately 10% for elderly hospitalised patients (8)

**References**
1. Nina Beyer et al. "In acutely admitted geriatric patients offering increased physical activity during hospitalization decreases length of stay and can improve mobility" (Nov. 2017)
2. Statistics Denmark, February 2019
3. J Appl Physiol 108: 1034–1040, 2010
4. J Am Geriatr Soc. 2003;51:451–458
5. Pedersen, Klarlund B, 2015
6. J Am Geriatr Soc. 2004;52:1263–1270
7. Lancet 2009;373:1874–1882, J Nutr Health Aging. 2016;20:738–751
8. Arch Phys Med Rehabil. September 2016 (Steve R. Fisher et al.)

                    ### Source Text
                    ## Case Highlight
title: Case Highlight

### Bispebjerg Hospital: Increased out-of-bed time of hospitalized patients
subtitle: Bispebjerg Hospital: Increased out-of-bed time of hospitalized patients
[View video](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/et-al._2-min-og-25-sek-of-fame-hell-yeah-altid-activity-6599636858651586560-oOmm/)

Bispebjerg Hospital have in a recent study increased time out of bed for elderly hospitalised patients by 51 minutes per day using SENS motion developed by the Danish start-up SENS Innovation

Many patients are inactive 18–20 hours a day in the hospital bed, and body functions decline rapidly during hospitalisation. Therefore, rehabilitation while hospitalised is crucial to avoid loss of independency, decline of quality of life and re-hospitalisation

References

[Business Insights: A patch and an app are getting patients out of bed](https://www.businessinsights.dk/healthtech/a-patch-and-an-app-are-getting-patients-out-of-bed/)
[European Journal of Internal Medicine: Dall CH, Andersen H, Povlsen TM, Henriksen M. Evaluation of a technology assisted physical activity intervention among hospitalised patients: A randomised study. Eur J Intern Med. 2019](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31494019/)

Project supported by the Association of Danish Physiotherapists, RegionH VihTek, Bispebjerg Hospital – Department of Physical and Occupational Therapy and SENS Innovation ApS

## Clinical Evidence
title: Clinical Evidence

### SENS motion® increases physical activity for admitted patients
subtitle: SENS motion® increases physical activity for admitted patients
list: Each year more than 600.000 elderly patients above the age of 65 are hospitalised (2)|Elderly hospitalised acute patients spend on average 70–90% of their time in bed and spend only 3–5% of their time either standing or walking (1)
Research has shown that even shorter periods of inactivity cause a reduction in physical fitness and muscle mass (3). Compared to younger patients, elderly patients have reduced ability to recover after periods of physical inactivity (4). This also affects the patient's ability to perform daily living (ADL) activities as well as degrading their perceived quality of life (5)

Physical inactivity is a strong factor prolonging the disease and rehabilitation period (7), increasing the risk of sequelae leading to hospital (re)admission (6). With an increase of just 100 steps taken per day the risk of readmission is reduced with approximately 10% for elderly hospitalised patients (8)

**References**
1. Nina Beyer et al. "In acutely admitted geriatric patients offering increased physical activity during hospitalization decreases length of stay and can improve mobility" (Nov. 2017)
2. Statistics Denmark, February 2019
3. J Appl Physiol 108: 1034–1040, 2010
4. J Am Geriatr Soc. 2003;51:451–458
5. Pedersen, Klarlund B, 2015
6. J Am Geriatr Soc. 2004;52:1263–1270
7. Lancet 2009;373:1874–1882, J Nutr Health Aging. 2016;20:738–751
8. Arch Phys Med Rehabil. September 2016 (Steve R. Fisher et al.)

                    ### Local Classes
                    - Service: A specific offering provided by a company or organization, typically to customers or clients.
- PrivacyPolicy: A legal document that outlines how an organization collects, uses, and manages an individual's data.
- MobileApplication: A software application that is designed to be executed, or at least partially executed, in a computer environment that also acts as a platform for a computer application.
- EmailAddress: A digital identifier that allows for electronic communication of messages between individuals, groups, and organizations.
- DataController: The entity that determines the purposes and conditions of the data processing activities. It is the 'owner' of the data and is responsible for ensuring that the data is used in compliance with all applicable data protection law.
- DataProcessor: An entity that processes data on behalf of the data controller, but does not have the same level of responsibility for data protection as the data controller.
- DataProcessingAgreement: A written agreement between a data controller and a data processor that outlines the specifics of data processing activities, including the nature and purpose of the processing, the types of data to be processed, and the intended use of the data.
- Email: A method of sending messages between sender and receiver, typically over the Internet using SMTP protocol.

                    ### Local Classes
                    - LegalBasis: The justification for processing personal data, as required by law or for specific purposes, such as contract performance or legitimate interests.

                    ### Source Text
                    ### Website: https://sens.dk

When you visit our website, we collect limited, aggregated usage data to understand how the website is used and to improve its performance

This includes
Pages visited
Referring websites
Device type, operating system, and browser

We do not use cookies or cross-site tracking technologies

**Legal basis:** Legitimate interest (GDPR Article 6(1)(f))
**Retention period:** Aggregated statistics are retained for up to 24 months

### Contact and Support

When you contact us via the website or through our support channels, we collect the information you provide, such as name, email address, and message content. We may also collect technical metadata such as IP address and browser user agent

This data is used solely to
Respond to enquiries
Provide customer support
Prevent spam and abuse

**Legal basis:** Legitimate interest (GDPR Article 6(1)(f))
**Retention period:** Up to 12 months after last contact, unless a longer retention period is required due to an ongoing customer relationship

### Mobile Applications and Activity Sensors

When using our mobile applications in combination with SENS motion or related activity sensors, the system collects measured activity data generated by the sensors

This data may include movement and activity measurements produced by sensor hardware. The data is used solely for the purposes defined by the specific healthcare, research, or service context in which the solution is deployed

Depending on the specific project or customer agreement

SENS Innovation ApS acts as **data processor**, processing activity data on behalf of a healthcare or research organisation acting as data controller, or
SENS Innovation ApS acts as **data controller**, where activity data is collected directly for service operation, development, or explicitly consented use

Where SENS Innovation ApS acts as a data processor, processing is governed by a written Data Processing Agreement

Activity data may, depending on context, constitute **health-related personal data** under GDPR Article 9. In such cases, processing is carried out in accordance with applicable GDPR requirements

We do not use activity data for marketing or profiling purposes

Depending on the application, we may also collect
Phone number (if you choose to log in)
Bluetooth name of the phone

We do **not** collect location data unless explicitly stated within a specific application

**Legal basis:**
Performance of a contract (GDPR Article 6(1)(b))
Explicit consent or applicable healthcare or research basis (GDPR Article 9(2)), where required

**Retention period:**
Account data: until account deletion or termination of the service
Activity data: according to instructions from the relevant data controller or applicable project agreements

## Legal Basis for Processing

We process personal data based on one or more of the following legal bases

**GDPR Article 6(1)(b)** – performance of a contract
**GDPR Article 6(1)(f)** – legitimate interests
**GDPR Article 9(2)(a)** – explicit consent, where applicable
**GDPR Article 9(2)(h) or (j)** – healthcare or scientific research purposes, where processing is carried out on behalf of a data controller

## Third-Party Service Providers

Access your personal data

Request erasure of your data
Restrict processing
Object to processing
Receive your data in a portable format (where applicable)

                    ### Local Classes
                    - HealthStudy: A research project focused on understanding and improving health outcomes, often involving the collection and analysis of data related to health and disease.
- MentalHealthResearch: A specific type of HealthStudy that focuses on the study of mental health, including the development of treatments and understanding of mental illnesses.
- ChildhoodDevelopmentStudy: A HealthStudy that specifically targets the study of physical, emotional, and social development during childhood.
- RiskAssessment: The process of identifying the potential for future adverse health outcomes in a population or individual, often used in the context of preventive medicine and public health.
- ResilienceAssessment: A component of RiskAssessment that evaluates an individual's ability to withstand and recover from adverse situations, particularly in the context of mental health.
- HealthIndicator: A measurable value that can be used to assess the health of an individual or population, often used in research studies to predict or monitor health outcomes.
- HereditaryCondition: A health condition that is influenced by genetic factors and can be studied in the context of its impact on mental health and development.
- PreventiveHealth: Health interventions and strategies aimed at preventing the onset of disease rather than treating symptoms, often informed by research studies.

                    ## Case Highlight
title: Case Highlight

### VIA 11 The Danish High Risk and Resilience Study
subtitle: VIA 11 The Danish High Risk and Resilience Study
**SENS motion® provides more detailed monitoring of more than 500 children, including activity and sleep pattern monitoring, to look for early warning indicators for psychiatric health.**

**About the project:**
The purpose of the VIA 11 study is to examine how your child feels when it is 11 years old. We want to gain better insight into how upbringing and hereditary conditions affect each other in relation to mental health. We want to shed light on the factors that give the child resistibility and resilience in relation to mental problems and illness. The results of the study can be used to increase the possibility of preventing mental illness in the future and to understand more about how mental illness occurs. Research is crucial for science to develop new treatments and gain a better understanding of disease and health

## Background

On the SENS motion app for smartphone and tablet, it is possible to assign a favorite sensor where it will always be possible to see the status of the favorite sensor on the front page of the app

By assigning a favorite sensor, it is possible to follow the progress of the sensor when data is transferred to the cloud from the sensor

## Select favorite sensor

To select a favorite sensor, follow these steps in the SENS motion app

Image: App screens showing favorite sensor selection step 1 (source: /img/support/app-smartphone/add-favorite-sensor/image1.png)
Image: App screens showing favorite sensor selection step 2 (source: /img/support/app-smartphone/add-favorite-sensor/image2.png)
Image: App screens showing favorite sensor selection step 3 (source: /img/support/app-smartphone/add-favorite-sensor/image3.png)
Image: App screens showing favorite sensor selection step 4 (source: /img/support/app-smartphone/add-favorite-sensor/image4.png)

The sensor will now be displayed on the front page of the app and you can keep track of how much data has been transferred

## Using the favorite sensor

The favorite sensor is displayed on the front page and is used to easily get an overview of how much data is on the sensor and how far in the transmission the sensor is

When the app is opened, the favorite sensor will show how much data is on the sensor (Image 1). The app will then start transferring data, showing how far the sensor is in downloading data (Image 2). When the app has finished transferring data, the sensor will be empty and there will only be a few seconds of data on the sensor (Image 3)

Image: Favorite sensor showing data on sensor (source: /img/support/app-smartphone/add-favorite-sensor/image5.png)
Image: Favorite sensor showing transfer in progress (source: /img/support/app-smartphone/add-favorite-sensor/image6.png)
Image: Favorite sensor showing data transferred (source: /img/support/app-smartphone/add-favorite-sensor/image7.png)

Image: SENS motion logo (source: /img/sens-motion-logo-black.png)

## Background

Each SENS motion sensor needs a smartphone app to communicate with the underlying system. This includes turning a sensor on and off, as well as pulling data out of the sensor

The app is designed to be installed both on a device the healthcare professional uses or on the patient's own phone

## Installation on iOS

The SENS motion app can be downloaded by searching for "SENS motion" in the App Store

Note that Bluetooth must be turned on on your phone and location services must be accepted the first time the app is opened

Image: SENS motion app on iOS (source: /img/support/app-smartphone/installation-of-the-sens-motion-smartphone-app/image1.png)

If Bluetooth and/or location services are not turned on, the Gateway Status on the main screen will be marked yellow and not green. You can see the number of sensors nearby under "Nearby"

The transfer of data from sensors is now automatic as long as the app is visible on the device

Pressing the "ADVANCED" button will display a list of nearby sensors. A favorite sensor can then be selected, the status of which will always be displayed on the front page. [See here how to add a favorite sensor](/support/app-smartphone/add-favorite-sensor)

## Installation on Android

The SENS motion app can be downloaded by searching for "SENS motion" in Google Play

Note that Bluetooth must be turned on on your phone and location services must be accepted the first time the app is opened

Image: SENS motion app on Android (source: /img/support/app-smartphone/installation-of-the-sens-motion-smartphone-app/image2.png)

If Bluetooth and/or location services are not turned on, the Gateway Status on the main screen will be marked yellow and not green. You can see the number of sensors nearby under "Nearby"

The transfer of data from sensors is now automatic as long as the app is visible on the device

Pressing the "ADVANCED" button will display a list of nearby sensors. A favorite sensor can then be selected, the status of which will always be displayed on the front page. [See here how to add a favorite sensor](/support/app-smartphone/add-favorite-sensor)

Image: SENS motion logo (source: /img/sens-motion-logo-black.png)

                    ### Local Classes
                    - SmartphoneApp: A mobile application designed to facilitate the monitoring and data transfer from sensors to a server, with features such as data transfer monitoring, favorite sensor functionality, and status indication.
- DataTransferProcess: The sequence of events and conditions involved in the transfer of data from a sensor to a server, including the necessary conditions for the process to occur, such as the proximity of the sensor to the device and the state of the application.
- SensorRangeRequirement: The specific physical range within which a sensor must be located relative to a device for data transfer to be successful, as determined by the application's functionality.
- DataTransferStatusIndicator: A visual or functional element within an application that provides real-time feedback on the status of data transfer operations, such as the number of sensors that are 'pending' or 'busy'.
- DataTransferCompletionCondition: The condition under which a data transfer process is considered complete, typically indicated by all sensors having no 'pending' or 'busy' status within the application.
- DataRetentionPolicy: The policy or feature within an application that dictates the behavior of unsent data when the application is closed during a data transfer process, such as saving and resuming the transfer at a later time.
- InstallationGuideReference: A reference to external documentation or instructions that provide guidance on how to install or set up an application, such as the SENS motion app, which is necessary for the data transfer process.

This article describes how to transfer data from the sensor to the server, making the data accessible in the web app ([app.sens.dk](https://app.sens.dk))

## Background

Data is transferred from the sensor to our server via the SENS motion app. The data becomes available in the web app once the transfer is complete

The SENS motion activity sensor will monitor and store all data for up to 10 days without needing to transfer data. Data is only transferred while the SENS motion app is open and within a range of 10–15 m of the sensor

Follow the guide below to transfer data

## How to transfer data from sensor to server

1. Open the SENS motion app on a smartphone or tablet. [See here for instructions on installing the SENS motion app](/support/app-smartphone/installation-of-the-sens-motion-smartphone-app)

When the SENS motion app is opened, the top block should be green as shown in the left image below. If the block is yellow as shown on the right, check that Bluetooth, data roaming, and location services are enabled. Restart your smartphone if necessary

2. The sensor(s) will automatically start transferring data as long as the SENS motion app is **open and running**, and the sensors are within 10–15 m of your device

3. Monitor the status of the data transfer for a specific sensor by setting a [favorite sensor](/support/app-smartphone/add-favorite-sensor). Transferring one day of data takes approximately 5 minutes. The more data on a sensor, the longer the transfer will take

Image: Gateway status green and yellow examples (source: /img/support/app-smartphone/transferring-data-from-sensor/image1.png)
Image: Gateway status with issues (source: /img/support/app-smartphone/transferring-data-from-sensor/image2.png)

Image: Status for transferring data from favorite sensor (source: /img/support/app-smartphone/transferring-data-from-sensor/image3.png)

Status for transferring data from favourite sensor

If you are transferring data for multiple sensors, you can monitor the status by seeing how many sensors are "pending" and "busy"

4. Once all data is transferred for your favorite sensor, the app will look like the image on the left. If you are transferring data for multiple sensors, the data transfer is complete when 0 sensors are "busy" and 0 sensors are "pending", as seen on the right image

Image: Data on favorite sensor has been transferred successfully (source: /img/support/app-smartphone/transferring-data-from-sensor/image4.png)

Data on favorite sensor has been transferred successfully

Image: Data for all sensors has been transferred successfully (source: /img/support/app-smartphone/transferring-data-from-sensor/image5.png)

Data for all sensores has been transferred successfully

**NOTE:** The sensor will stop transferring data if the SENS motion application is closed or running in the background on the smartphone. It is important to keep the app open while transferring data

If the SENS motion app is closed during the process, the sensor will save the data that was not transferred and resume the transfer next time the app is opened

                    ### Local Classes
                    - DataSecurityArticle: A written work that discusses the data security aspects and compliance of a specific technology or system.
- LegalDocument: A document that outlines the legal requirements and agreements necessary for data processing and storage, often used in the context of technology and data protection.
