                    ### Local Classes
                    - SoftwareRelease: A specific version of a software application that introduces new features, improvements, and bug fixes.
- SoftwareFeature: A particular capability or function provided by a software application, such as improved navigation, sensor management, or file handling.
- SoftwareFunctionality: The operational aspects of a software application, including tasks like monitoring, exporting, and managing within the application.
- SoftwareUIElement: An element of the user interface of a software application, such as breadcrumbs, navigation sections, or memory status indicators.
- SoftwareUIImprovement: Enhancements made to the user interface of a software application to improve usability, such as a more intuitive navigation structure or streamlined interface.
- SoftwareSecurityFeature: Security-related functionalities within a software application, including two-factor authentication and stricter password policies.
- SoftwareSupport: The range of countries or regions that a software application supports, which can be expanded or reduced based on user needs.
- SoftwareOrganization: The structure within a software application that allows for the organization of projects, participants, and files, often reflected in dedicated sections or dedicated features.
- SoftwareOrganizationMenu: A specific feature within a software application that allows users to switch between different organizations or groups.
- SoftwareNavigation: The system or feature within a software application that allows users to move between different sections or views, such as a main navigation or breadcrumb trail.
- SoftwareNavigationFeature: A specific feature within a software application that aids in navigation, such as breadcrumbs or a dedicated navigation section.
- SoftwareNavigationSection: A dedicated section within a software application's main navigation that groups related features or tools for easier access and management.
- SoftwareNavigationSectionNewFeature: A newly introduced feature within a software application's navigation that reorganizes or enhances the way users access and interact with the application.
- SoftwareNavigationSectionNewFeature: A new feature within a software application's navigation that provides a more intuitive way to access and manage related features or tools.
- SoftwareNavigationSectionNewFeature: A new feature within a software application's navigation that reorganizes or enhances the way users access and interact with the application.

                    ### Source Text
                    We're excited to introduce version 3.9 with major improvements to navigation, sensor management, and file handling. This release focuses on making your workflow more efficient and providing better visibility into your sensor data What's New in 3.9 Reorganised Navigation – Three main sections (Workspace, Sensors, Files) for easier access to key features Enhanced Breadcrumbs – Quick navigation between project and participant views Improved Sensor Overview – Battery level info, sensor state, pending actions, and bulk operations Battery Lifetime Estimation – See estimated days remaining for each sensor battery Streamlined File Export – Export multiple files without closing the menu Expandable Participant Tables – View detailed information without leaving the table Memory Status Indicators – Quickly identify sensors with memory issues ActiMotus 2.1 Algorithm Support – Multi-sensor measurements and enhanced diary features Participant Details Enhancements – New Steps section added Security Improvements – Stricter password policies and enhanced two-factor authentication Expanded Country Support – More countries available for selection Streamlined UI – Overall interface improvements for better usability Reorganised Main Navigation Sections New Feature We've reorganised the main navigation to make it easier to find what you need Three dedicated sections make up the main navigation Workspace – Manage your projects and participants all in one place Sensors – View and manage all your sensors with enhanced battery and status information Files – Monitor and download your exported files What you need to know These sections replace the previous navigation structure and provide a more intuitive way to access your most-used features. You'll find all project and participant management tools in Workspace, sensor monitoring in Sensors, and file exports in Files. Additionally, the change organisation menu is now located directly in the header, next to profile settings, making it quicker to switch between organisations Enhanced Navigation with Breadcrumbs New Feature Breadcrumb navigation has been added throughout the application to help you understand where you are and quickly navigate between related views What you need to know When viewing project or participant details, you'll see breadcrumbs at the top showing your current location. You can click on any breadcrumb to jump directly to that section—for example, from a participant's detail page back to the participant overview or project list. This makes it much faster to navigate between related pages without using the back button How to use it Simply click on any item in the breadcrumb trail to navigate to that level. The breadcrumbs automatically update based on your current location Sensor Overview Improvements Battery Level Information New Feature The sensor overview now displays comprehensive battery information to help you monitor sensor lifetime Each sensor now shows Battery voltage – The current voltage reading from the sensor (when hovering over the icon) Estimated days left – A calculation of how many days of battery life remain based on the sensor's runtime and battery What you need to know The estimated days left is calculated based on how long the sensor has been active and its current battery voltage. This helps you plan ahead for sensor replacements and ensures you don't lose data due to depleted batteries How to use it Look for the battery icon and information under each sensor in the Sensors section. The estimated days left will help you identify sensors that need attention soon. To see battery voltage, hover the mouse cursor over the battery icon Sensor State and Pending Actions New Feature The sensor overview now clearly displays both the current sensor state and any pending actions What you need to know Sensor State shows what the sensor is currently doing (on, off, etc.) based on the last time it connected to the mobile app Pending Action shows any requested action (like "turn on" or "turn off") that is waiting for the sensor to connect to the mobile app This dual display helps you understand both the current status and what will happen next when the sensor syncs How to use it Check the sensor state column to see current status, and the pending action column to see what's queued. Pending actions will be completed automatically when the sensor connects to the SENS motion mobile app Improved Sensor Action Accessibility New Feature Turning sensors on and off is

You'll find all project and participant management tools in Workspace, sensor monitoring in Sensors, and file exports in Files.                     - DataStorageRequirement: A condition or need for a device or system to store data, which, when reached, necessitates synchronization to free up space.
- DataCollectionPeriod: A specific time frame during which data is gathered or recorded, often used to organize and reference data collection events.
- DataFilteringFeature: A functionality that allows users to sift through data based on certain criteria, such as date ranges or specific attributes.
- DataCorrelationTool: A tool or feature that enables the association of different data points, such as sensor readings and diary entries, to provide a more comprehensive analysis.
- DataAnalysisTool: A system or application feature that processes and interprets data, often to provide insights or to support decision-making.
- DataPrivacyPolicy: A set of rules and guidelines that govern the collection, storage, and sharing of personal data to ensure user privacy and compliance with regulations.
- DataSecurityFeature: Technological measures implemented to protect data from unauthorized access, alteration, or theft, often including encryption and access controls.
- DataSynchronizationProcess: The act of making sure that data across different systems or devices is consistent and up-to-date, often necessary when storage capacity is full.
- DataRetentionPolicy: A policy that defines how long data should be kept before it is disposed of or archived, balancing the need for historical records against storage costs and privacy concerns.
- DataIntegrityCheck: The process of verifying that data has not been tampered with and is accurate and reliable for use in decision-making or analysis.
- DataVisualizationTool: A software tool that converts data into a visual format, such as graphs or charts, to make it easier to understand and interpret.
- DataEntryTask: The act of manually or automatically inputting data into a system, which can be a critical step in the data collection and analysis process.
- DataEntryError: An error or discrepancy that occurs during the data entry process, which can lead to inaccurate data analysis if not identified and corrected.
- DataEntryValidation: The process of checking data for accuracy and completeness before it is entered into a system, to ensure the integrity of the data collected.
- DataEntryInterface: The user interface elements that facilitate the entry of data into a system, which can include forms, fields, and input fields.
- DataEntryGuidelines: Standards or best practices that guide the process of entering data into a system, aimed at maintaining consistency and accuracy.
- DataEntryTool: A specific application or software that assists users in entering data into a system, often designed to streamline the process and reduce errors.
- DataEntryProcedure: The method or set of steps followed when entering data into a system, which can vary depending on the system and the nature of the data.
- DataEntryProcess: The overall process of collecting, entering, and managing data within a system, from initial collection to final storage and analysis.
- DataEntryRequirement: The necessity for certain data to be entered into a system, which can be a critical component of data collection and analysis.
- DataEntryRequirement: A specific need or demand for data entry, which can drive the design and implementation of data collection systems.
- DataEntryRequirement: A formal need for data to be entered into a system, often based on regulatory, business, or research requirements.

                    ### Local Classes
                    - ApplicationFeature: A specific functionality or component within a software application that provides a particular service or capability to the user.
- SecurityFeature: A set of measures or capabilities within a system designed to protect data, ensure privacy, and prevent unauthorized access.
- UserInterface: The means by which a person interacts with a digital device or a software application, including the display, input, and output elements.
- UserFeedback: Comments, suggestions, or evaluations provided by users regarding their experience with a product or service, often used to inform future development and improvements.
- DataCollectionTool: A method or system used to gather information, typically from users or participants, for analysis or research purposes.
- UserProfile: A digital representation of a user's personal information and preferences within an application, often used for customization and personalization of services.
- UserProfileFeature: A specific aspect or component of a UserProfile that enhances the user's experience by providing additional functionality or personalization.

                    ### Local Classes
                    - SensorState: The current operational condition of a sensor, such as 'on' or 'off', reflecting its last known activity.

                    ### Source Text
                    Lead: [](https://support.sens.dk/hc/en-us/articles/32478651584925-Web-App-3-9-Release-Notes)

## Background

Each sensor can be in different 'states'. In order for the sensor to be turned on and ready for monitoring, the sensor must first be turned on and be synced with the SENS motion app

The sensor overview clearly displays both the current sensor state and any pending actions
Image: Sensor state (source: /img/support/web-application/turn-sensor-on-off/pending-action-status.png)

**Sensor State** shows what the sensor is currently doing (on, off, etc.) based on the last time it connected to the SENS motion app
**Pending Action** shows any requested action (like "turn on" or "turn off") that is waiting for the sensor to connect to the SENS motion app

This dual display helps you understand both the current status and what will happen next when the sensor syncs with the SENS motion app
Check the sensor state column to see current status, and the pending action column to see what's queued

Pending actions will be completed automatically when the sensor connects to the participant's mobile app

We estimate how long a sensor's battery should last (default about 120 days)
We track how long it has been running and when it was last seen. If it's still running, time since last seen is counted against the battery
If we have a recent voltage reading, we adjust the remaining days based on voltage thresholds. This is so we can get a more realistic estimate, in case something has happened to the battery. Very low voltage forces the remaining days to near-zero; normal voltage leaves the estimate as-is
If the sensor hasn't been seen in a very long time (years) or has run far beyond its expected lifetime, we show **"Battery expired"** in UI
If the battery data is older than 10 days, we determine voltage is "unknown.", and only show the estimated days left (not the voltage) in the UI

## How to turn on the sensor

You can either turn the sensors on, one by one *or* in bulk. See both ways below

1. Log on to [https://app.sens.dk/r/login](https://app.sens.dk/r/login)

                    ### Local Classes
                    - BulkAction: An action performed on multiple items or entities simultaneously, as opposed to a single action.
- App: A software application designed to perform specific tasks on a mobile device or tablet, such as the SENS motion app mentioned in the source text.
- SensorStatus: The current operational state of a sensor, which can be 'On', 'Off', or 'Pending action' as described in the source text.
- SensorActivation: The process of activating a sensor, which may involve physical actions such as shaking the sensor to connect it with an application.
- SensorManagement: The process of turning sensors on or off, which can be done individually or in bulk as described in the source text.

                    ### Source Text
                    ### Turn on sensors one by one

2. Enter the "Sensors" tab, click the three dots on the far right and click "Turn sensor on"

Image: Turn on sensor one by one (source: /img/support/web-application/turn-sensor-on-off/turn-single-sensor-on.png)

### Turn on several sensors at once (bulk)

2. Click "Bulk actions", and select the sensors you want to turn on, followed by clicking "Turn on the selected sensors"

Image: Turn on several sensors at once (bulk) (source: /img/support/web-application/turn-sensor-on-off/turn-bulk-sensors-on.png)

3. The sensors will change under "Pending action" to

Image: Pending action status (source: /img/support/web-application/turn-sensor-on-off/pending-action-on.png)

4. Open the SENS motion app on your mobile/tablet (accept bluetooth and location services when you download first time)

Image: Open SENS motion app (source: /img/support/web-application/turn-sensor-on-off/sens-motion-app.jpg)

5. Shake the sensor a few times to activate the accelerometer to connect the sensor with the SENS motion app

6. Check on the website if the status has changed to "On"

Image: Sensor status changed to on (source: /img/support/web-application/turn-sensor-on-off/check-sensor-status.png)

To turn off the sensors, it is exactly the same procedure, but by clicking "Turn sensor off" instead

To save battery life time, it is important to turn off the sensors after use

Warning: **NOTE:** The sensor is only switched on when the status changes to "On" and only turned off when the state indicates "Off"

### Goal
Extract specific data values and attributes for the given Individuals based on the provided Data Property Axioms and Source Text.

### Guidelines
1. **Strict Subjects**: You MUST ONLY use the exact 'id's of the provided 'Declared Individuals' for "subject".
2. **Strict Properties**: You MUST ONLY use the exact properties defined in the 'Valid Data Axioms'.
3. **Datatypes**: Assign the appropriate XSD datatype (e.g., xsd:string, xsd:boolean, xsd:integer).
4. **Actual Values**: The `value` field must contain the actual data from the text (e.g., "19.995", "120", "John"). Do NOT put the XSD type (e.g., "xsd:decimal") into the value field. 
4. **Empty on Absent**: Return an empty array `[]` if no valid links exist.

### Example
[
  {"subject": "Main Server", "property": "uptimeHours", "value": "720", "datatype": "xsd:integer"},
  {"subject": "John Doe", "property": "emailAddress", "value": "john.doe@example.com", "datatype": "xsd:string"}
]<|end|><|user|>
                    ### Declared Individuals
                    - SENS motion app (Class: App)

                    ### Valid Data Axioms
                    - BulkAction -> isPerformed -> xsd:boolean
- App -> isInstalled -> xsd:boolean
- SensorStatus -> isActive -> xsd:boolean
- SensorActivation -> requiresAction -> xsd:boolean
- SensorManagement -> canPerformBulkAction -> xsd:boolean
- SensorWarning -> isDisplayed -> xsd:boolean

                    ### Source Text
                    ### Turn on sensors one by one

2. Enter the "Sensors" tab, click the three dots on the far right and click "Turn sensor on"

Image: Turn on sensor one by one (source: /img/support/web-application/turn-sensor-on-off/turn-single-sensor-on.png)

### Turn on several sensors at once (bulk)

2. Click "Bulk actions", and select the sensors you want to turn on, followed by clicking "Turn on the selected sensors"

Image: Turn on several sensors at once (bulk) (source: /img/support/web-application/turn-sensor-on-off/turn-bulk-sensors-on.png)

3. The sensors will change under "Pending action" to

Image: Pending action status (source: /img/support/web-application/turn-sensor-on-off/pending-action-on.png)

4. Open the SENS motion app on your mobile/tablet (accept bluetooth and location services when you download first time)

Image: Open SENS motion app (source: /img/support/web-application/turn-sensor-on-off/sens-motion-app.jpg)

5. Shake the sensor a few times to activate the accelerometer to connect the sensor with the SENS motion app

6. Check on the website if the status has changed to "On"

Image: Sensor status changed to on (source: /img/support/web-application/turn-sensor-on-off/check-sensor-status.png)

To turn off the sensors, it is exactly the same procedure, but by clicking "Turn sensor off" instead

To save battery life time, it is important to turn off the sensors after use

Warning: **NOTE:** The sensor is only switched on when the status changes to "On" and only turned off when the state indicates "Off"

                    ### Goal
                    Extract specific data values and attributes for the given Individuals based on the Original Text and Valid Data Axioms.<|end|><|assistant|>

A newly introduced feature within a software application's navigation that reorganizes or enhances the way users access and interact with the application.
A new feature or improvement added to a system, application, or service that enhances its functionality or user experience.
A feature that allows users to apply changes or perform actions on a selection of items at once, typically found in software applications for bulk data management.
A feature that provides an estimation of the remaining battery life for a device or sensor, often used to predict when a device will need recharging or replacement.
A process or set of features related to the exporting of files, which may include batch operations or streamlined user interfaces to facilitate the transfer of data.
Enhancements or new features in project management tools that simplify the creation, organization, and tracking of projects, often aimed at improving user experience and efficiency.
Improvements or additional features added to a table or data table interface that provide more information or functionality, such as the ability to expand rows for more details.

A condition or need for a device or system to store data, which, when reached, necessitates synchronization to free up space.
A specific time frame during which data is gathered or recorded, often used to organize and reference data collection events.
A functionality that allows users to sift through data based on certain criteria, such as date ranges or specific attributes.
A tool or feature that enables the association of different data points, such as sensor readings and diary entries, to provide a more comprehensive analysis.
A system or application feature that processes and interprets data, often to provide insights or to support decision-making.
A set of rules and guidelines that govern the collection, storage, and sharing of personal data to ensure user privacy and compliance with regulations.
Technological measures implemented to protect data from unauthorized access, alteration, or theft, often including encryption and access controls.
The act of making sure that data across different systems or devices is consistent and up-to-date, often necessary when storage capacity is full.
The process of verifying that data has not been tampered with and is accurate and reliable for use in decision-making or analysis.
A software tool that converts data into a visual format, such as graphs or charts, to make it easier to understand and interpret.

Comments, suggestions, or evaluations provided by users regarding their experience with a product or service, often used to inform future development and improvements.

axioms.description: A hierarchical connection indicating that one class is a sub-type or child of another class.
Relates an entity to its components or parts. (e.g., A car hasPart an engine).
The inverse of hasPart. Relates a component to the whole. (e.g., An engine isPart of a car).
Relates an object to an event or process it is involved in. (e.g., A person participatesIn a meeting).
The inverse of participatesIn. Relates an event to the objects involved in it. (e.g., A meeting hasParticipant a person).
Relates an entity to an attribute, characteristic, or value it possesses. (e.g., A car hasQuality color).
Relates a physical manifestation to the abstract information it represents. (e.g., A printed book realizes a text; A physical statue realizes a 3D design).
Relates a concept, category, or role to the actual entity it describes. (e.g., The role 'Teacher' classifies a specific Person).
Relates an object or event to the spatial region where it is located or occurs. (e.g., A meeting hasLocation 'Conference Room A').
Relates an event or process to the specific time period during which it occurs. (e.g., A concert hasTime 'Saturday Night').
Relates an information object (like data, text, or a picture) to the entity it represents or describes. (e.g., A photograph isAbout a landscape).
Relates a complex system or platform to its individual components. (e.g., A computer hasComponent a motherboard).
The inverse of hasComponent. Relates a component to the system it belongs to. (e.g., A motherboard isComponentOf a computer).
A sequence relation indicating that one event, process, or time happens before another. (e.g., 'Writing' precedes 'Publishing').
The inverse of precedes. Indicates one event or process comes after another. (e.g., 'Publishing' follows 'Writing').
Relates an object to the function or role it performs. (e.g., A person hasRole 'Manager').
A data property that assigns a specific numeric or literal value to an entity or its quality. (e.g., A WeightQuality hasDataValue '75.5').
Relates a collection, dataset, or group to the individual items it contains. (e.g., A Library hasMember 'Book').
The inverse of hasMember. Relates an item to the collection it belongs to. (e.g., A 'Book' isMemberOf a Library).
Relates a piece of information or data to the abstract concept it means or represents. (e.g., A spoken word expresses a concept).
Direct precedence indicating a requirement. Relates an action to another action or event that must happen first. (e.g., 'Buying a Ticket' is a precondition for 'Boarding a Train').
Direct succession indicating a result. Relates an action to the outcome or event that necessarily follows it. (e.g., 'Getting Wet' is a postcondition of 'Swimming').
Relates an action or physical event to the specific conceptual task or goal it is designed to accomplish. (e.g., A reading event executesTask 'Learning').
Relates an entity to the medium or tool it uses to interact or perform an action. (e.g., A writer actsThrough a 'Pen').
Relates an entity or action to a rule, plan, or requirement that it successfully fulfills. (e.g., A building process satisfies a 'Blueprint').
States that two classes are semantically identical and share the exact same instances.

ontology_abox.subject: VIA 11
ontology_abox.value: to examine how your child feels when it is 11 years old. We want to gain better insight into how upbringing and hereditary conditions affect each other in relation to mental health. We want to shed light on the factors that give the child resistibility and resilience in relation to mental problems and illness. The results of the study can be used to increase the possibility of preventing mental illness in the future and to understand more about how mental illness occurs. Research is crucial for science to develop new treatments and gain a better understanding of disease and health

ontology_abox.subject: SENS motion®

ontology_abox.subject: SENS motion for Healthcare
ontology_abox.value: Patients will be provided with a tablet in the room where the motivation app is installed. The app will motivate patients to better self-mobilize by giving them a guided virtual tour of Copenhagen. On the app, patients can follow their own activity overview, see how far in the tour they have come, the number of steps and how much active time they have had, both that day and in the last week

SENS motion-webapp-39-release-notes
ontology_abox.value: place one hand against the skin to provide support for the skin while the patch is gently pulled off along the leg. Do not pull away from the skin, but instead pull along the skin. To ease removal, it is recommended to use a skin lotion to help loosen the adhesive. The patch typically begins to loosen itself after 10–14 days
